steps.title: Test equation one
steps.content:
Multiply the original equation by $1.5$.
$$
1.5(7x+5y)=1.5(2)
$$
$$
10.5x+7.5y=3
$$
Equation I is the same line, so it gives infinitely many solutions.

steps.title: Test equation two
steps.content:
The original line has slope 
$$
-\frac{7}{5}
$$ Rewrite equation II.
$$
10.5x-7.5y=3
$$
$$
y=\frac{7}{5}x-\frac{2}{5}
$$
The slopes are different, so the lines intersect once.

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content: Graphing the original equation with equations I and II shows that equation I overlaps the original line, while equation II crosses it once. Both equations could be the other equation. The answer is C.
